pi_items.source: seed_demodb.py line 69: 'CREATE CLASS Hotels EXTENDS V'; verifier.py lines 54-58 verify Hotels class exists
task.json description (line 5) explicitly states 'Create a new property named Location'; setup_task.sh line 22 drops Hotels.Location IF EXISTS; verifier.py lines 62-73 check for Location property

OrientDB 3.2.36 documentation (https://orientdb.dev/docs/develop/indexing/Spatial-Index.html) confirms OPoint as valid spatial geometry type; verifier.py line 66 checks: location_prop.get('type') == 'OPoint'

OrientDB 3.2.36 documentation (https://orientdb.dev/docs/develop/indexing/Spatial-Index.html) confirms SPATIAL as valid index type using Lucene engine; verifier.py line 77 checks: idx.get('type') == 'SPATIAL'

privileged_info_summary: The spatial_migration_and_cleanup task operates on the 'demodb' travel agency database, specifically the Hotels class. The task requires creation of a Location property of type OPoint (a point geometry type in OrientDB) with a SPATIAL index for geospatial queries. The Hotels class contains 15 real-world hotel records (Hotel Artemide in Rome, Hotel Adlon Kempinski in Berlin, Hotel de Crillon in Paris, The Savoy in London, The Plaza Hotel in New York, Park Hyatt Tokyo in Tokyo, Four Seasons Sydney in Sydney, Copacabana Palace in Rio de Janeiro, Hotel Arts Barcelona in Barcelona, Grande Bretagne Hotel in Athens, Intercontinental Amsterdam in Amsterdam, Fairmont Le Manoir in Paris, Hotel Villa d Este in Cernobbio, Baglioni Hotel Luna in Venice, and Melia Berlin in Berlin). Each hotel record includes real latitude/longitude coordinates that must be migrated to the new Location property. The environment uses OrientDB Community Edition 3.2.36.
